rationale: The signals show strong, rising search demand for specific genetics and breeding topics (Cappuccino, sexing, Tiger morphs). Google Trends reveals +110% surge in "how to sex a crested gecko" and +80% for "tiger crested gecko", with multiple breeder videos expressing frustration around Cappuccino breeding outcomes. These align with existing knowledge in our genetics glossary (incomplete dominance, polygenic traits). I rejected general care topics, listicles ("morph chart"), and duplicates of already-published slugs. The three topics picked have concrete misconceptions breeders can act on and are grounded in verifiable genetics mechanics.
